name: Walter Booker
abstract: Walter Booker (December 17, 1933 – November 24, 2006) was an American jazz musician. A native of Prairie View, Texas, his playing was marked by voice-like inflections, glissandos and tremolo techniques.

interviews.title: Walter Booker and Jimmy Cobb interviewed by Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 30, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: the trio that I had there: Walter Booker on bass and Jimmy Cobbs (sic) on drums and John Hicks on piano

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: So next: Don Pullen, Walter Booker on bass and Milford on drums. So everybody's sitting there and I'm sitting there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Horn Shirley
about_quotes.evidence: Walter Booker, Billy Hart were in the band. ... Walter Booker just stayed with me that three weeks.
about_quotes.doc_title: Horn Shirley — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Cranshaw
about_quotes.evidence: I started to send other bass players when I started to work Sesame Street after a while, I’d © Fillius Jazz Archive -10- send in Walter Booker and a lot of other guys who also enjoyed the experience that I was having working with this group of guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Cranshaw interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Turbinton
about_quotes.evidence: I stayed in New York for a period of time with wonderful hospitality given to me by Walter Booker and enabled me to get acclimated to New York and get into the New York scene

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: it was Sarah and a trio with Carl Drinkert and Walter Booker and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
